scenarios.title: search filters the employee grid to show matching rows @positive
scenarios.userAction: Type 'Jane' in the employee search box and observe the grid filter to rows whose name contains 'jane'.
expectedAssertions: employee grid has at least one row visible after typing 'Jane'
first visible row's name cell contains 'jane' (case-insensitive)

scenarios.title: inviting a new employee shows a confirmation toast @positive
scenarios.userAction: Click the Add/Invite button, enter 'new.hire@beacon.test' in the invite modal, submit, and observe a confirmation toast.
expectedAssertions: invite modal dialog appears after clicking the Add button
confirmation toast with text 'Invitation sent' is visible after submitting the email

locatorTable.notes: Direct ID→CSS mapping per KB §6 Rule 1. Upgrade to getByLabel(/search/i) if DOM exposes a <label for='search-employees'>.
XPath targets <button> tag (semantic evidence) with 'Add' text. Exact accessible name unverified — Q3. See pin 1.
CSS-class-only evidence. Modal role='dialog' and button name are guessed — Q4. See pin 2.
Descriptive structural CSS selector. No accessible row name available. May degrade if class renamed — Q7. See pin 6.
Positional first() retained. No accessible cell name evidence — Q7. See pin 3.
No label evidence for email input. Assumes single textbox in modal — Q5. See pin 4.
Toast ARIA role unverified. span[2] positional index loses meaning; use toContainText rather than toHaveText until Q6 resolved — Q6. See pin 5.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q3 unresolved: accessible name of add-employee header button not confirmed — assumed /add/i covers all variants.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Inspect header DOM, confirm element tag + full accessible name, then tighten the name regex or switch to exact string.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q4 unresolved: modal role='dialog' and send-button accessible name not confirmed.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Ask FE team to confirm ARIA role on modal wrapper and exact visible text on the send button. If modal lacks role='dialog', scope via page.locator('.invite-modal') instead.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q7 unresolved: grid DOM structure (table vs div) not confirmed — positional first() retained as fallback.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If grid is semantic <table> with <tr>/<td>, replace with page.getByRole('row').nth(1).getByRole('cell', { name: ... }) for a role-based upgrade.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q5 unresolved: email input label not confirmed — assumed single textbox in modal.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If input has a visible label, use getByLabel(/email/i). If placeholder only, use getByPlaceholder(...). Both are more specific and survive modal refactors that add more inputs.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q6 unresolved: toast ARIA role not confirmed. span[2] positional index dropped; toContainText used instead of toHaveText to tolerate icon spans.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Ask FE team to add role='alert' (urgent) or role='status' (polite) to the toast component. If neither is feasible, fall back to page.locator('div.toast').filter({ hasText: /invitation sent/i }).

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q7 unresolved: grid DOM structure not confirmed — CSS class selector retained for count/filter assertions.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If grid is a semantic <table>, use page.getByRole('row') filtered to exclude the header row (e.g., .filter({ hasNotText: /^name$/i })).
